persona.name: Aaliyah Washington

occupation.title: Content Marketing Coordinator
occupation.description: Coordinates social media content and email campaigns for a Black-owned sustainable consumer goods company in Atlanta. Writes product copy, manages Instagram and TikTok accounts, and researches ethical brand partnerships. Passionate about the company's mission and applies the same values lens to her personal purchases.

persona.education: B.A. in Communications with a Marketing concentration from Spelman College, class of 2024. Graduated with honors. Led a senior-year panel on ethical consumerism and was active in Spelman's entrepreneurship club.
long_term_goals: Launch her own sustainable beauty brand focused on melanin-rich skin tones by age 30
Buy a condo in West Midtown Atlanta within five years
Grow her personal Instagram to 50K as a values-driven skincare creator
Travel to at least three African countries to study their beauty and skincare traditions
Build generational wealth through financial literacy and early investing
persona.style: Aaliyah is warm, intentional, and articulate. She brings energy to every conversation and holds strong opinions she expresses with confidence, not aggression. She speaks in complete, considered sentences and backs claims with specifics — 'I stopped buying from them because their palm oil sourcing isn't RSPO certified.' Her aesthetic is Afrocentric-modern: bold prints, natural hair, minimal but deliberate makeup. Her skincare shelfie is curated and photogenic.

traits: Values-driven — ethical sourcing and brand accountability are non-negotiable
Warm and community-oriented; believes in lifting others up
Confident and opinionated but genuinely curious and open to counter-evidence
Research-oriented: reads founder interviews and supply chain reports before buying
Entrepreneurial mindset; constantly evaluating products as a potential founder
Fiercely proud of Black identity; actively supports Black-owned businesses

interests: Ethical and sustainable beauty brands (Fenty Skin, Topicals, Hyper Skin)
Black-owned business discovery, support, and amplification
Natural hair care: protective styling, deep conditioning, shea-based products
Social media marketing trends and content strategy
African and Caribbean beauty ingredients: shea butter, black seed oil, baobab
Entrepreneurship podcasts and startup business books
Vegan cooking and local farmers markets
Community organizing and Spelman alumnae network
likes: Hyperpigmentation products formulated and tested for melanin-rich skin
Brands that publicly share supply chain and ingredient sourcing
Black female founders in the beauty industry
Skincare that celebrates rather than tries to erase melanin
Vitamin C and tranexamic acid for dark spot treatment
Her natural hair routine with shea butter and castor oil
Brunch at Black-owned restaurants in Atlanta
dislikes: Brands that profit from Black consumers without reinvesting in Black communities
Greenwashing — vague sustainability claims without third-party certification
Generic 'for all skin tones' marketing that doesn't actually test on deeper complexions
Plastic-heavy packaging from brands claiming eco-consciousness

beliefs: Beauty products should work for every skin tone, not just the lightest ones
Where you spend your money is a political act
Representation in beauty advertising changes how Black girls see themselves
Sustainability and profitability are not mutually exclusive — they reinforce each other
Generational wealth starts with small, consistent financial decisions every day
skills: Content marketing and social media strategy (Instagram, TikTok)
Brand ethics auditing and supply chain research
Skincare efficacy evaluation for deeper melanin-rich skin tones

general: Researches a brand's founder story, sourcing, and community investment before purchasing
Posts weekly values-based skincare reviews on her Instagram stories
Maintains a Notion database of vetted vs. boycotted brands
Gifts skincare to her mom and cousins as a way to share knowledge and care

morning: 6:30am wake-up, gratitude journal while drinking green tea
Gentle cleanse with Caudalie Micellar Water
Good Molecules Tranexamic Acid serum, tinted SPF 50 moisturizer
Business podcast during commute to her WeWork coworking space
workday: Remote work from home or a café in Kirkwood neighborhood
Morning content planning, afternoon deep-work writing sessions
Lunch at a local Black-owned restaurant when she leaves the house
Afternoon hydrating mist for a dewy midday refresh
evening: Home by 6:30pm; yoga or home cardio 4x/week
Double cleanse: balm cleanser + foam cleanser
Essence, Hyper Skin Vitamin C serum, Topicals Faded treatment, moisturizer, occlusive
Scroll #SkincareAddiction or work on her brand business plan
weekend: Sunday Ponce City Market for local vendors and Black-owned finds
Saturday morning: deep conditioning hair treatment
Brunch with Spelman friends at a Black-owned restaurant
2 hours on her skincare brand business plan

health.physical: Healthy and active. Primary skincare concern is post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) from past breakouts; manages with targeted serums rather than prescription treatments. Practices yoga and cardio regularly. Mostly plant-based diet.
health.mental: Emotionally resilient and grounded. Occasionally stretched thin from work plus side projects, but maintains balance through journaling, community, and faith. Active member of a Black Baptist church in Decatur.

relationships.name: Sandra Washington
relationships.description: Works as a school counselor in Decatur. Aaliyah's biggest supporter and first call for life advice. They bond over cooking; Aaliyah has converted her to a cleaner skincare routine.

relationships.name: Destiny Clarke
relationships.description: Now a law student at Howard. They talk daily and swap skincare recommendations over text. Destiny will be Aaliyah's first beta tester when the brand launches.

relationships.name: Marcus Webb
relationships.description: 26-year-old data analyst she's been dating 14 months. He doesn't care about skincare but respects her passion. They're discussing moving in together.
